title: Dirilis: Ertugrul
transcript_text:
Many web series or tv series gathered a huge
audience due to the lockdown. One of them is Dirilis Ertugrul which gathered
huge fame. Dirilis Ertugrul is a great web series that
is created by Mehmet Bozdag. The tv series stars Engin Altan Duzyatan in
the lead role. The show has gained major popularity in Pakistan
and Turkey. The series is based on the life of Ertugrul
who founded the Ottoman Empire. The show is uploaded on Netflix which a person
can watch, while the Tv series of this show is currently going on. Plot Summary of Dirilis Ertugrul The plot summary of the Dirilis Ertugrul focuses
on the life of Ertugrul who was a famous warrior from 13th century. Ertugrul was also the father of Osman, the
man who founded the famous Ottoman Empire. The plot follows the life of Ertugrul who
is a peace and justice-loving person. The show has a different season and each season
discusses his struggle with the Crusaders, Byzantines and the Mongols who keeps on killing
many Turkish people to expand their empire. The show depicts how he fought all of his
enemies and proclaimed his destiny. Summary of the Ottoman Empire The Ottoman Empire was a huge empire that
ruled over south-eastern Europe, Western Asia, and northern Africa. The empire was established in the 13th century
and continued until the early 20th century. Starting as a tribe, the empire was founded
by Osman I, and under his rule, the tribe grew well conquering various estates and countries. The name of the empire was chosen as the Ottoman
which is the historical anglicization of the word Osman I, the man who founded the empire. The empire rule is considered to be one of
the longest rules in the world lasting over 6 centuries. The empire capital was chosen as Constantinople
and they rule their estates from the capital. The empire ruled for 6 centuries and it saw
fall and rise of many rulers out of which the most famous one is Osman I. A modern-day tv-series Dirilis Ertugrul is
broadcasted all over the world showing the life of Ertugrul (the father of Osman I) and
his son Osman as they set up the Ottoman empire. Reason for its Popularity The show is popular all around the world and
is gain huge popularity in the Arabic nation. The show is popular in Turkey and Pakistan
where many people are watching it every day. The prime minister of Pakistan, Mr. Imran
Khan, insisted that everyone in their country should watch the famous series and since then
the show gathered a huge fan base in Pakistan. This isn’t the first time that Turkish tv
series has become famous in Pakistan. There are various Tv shows such as Ask-i Memnu
and Fatmagul (Fatmagul'un Sucu Ne?) that have gathered a huge fan base in Pakistan as well. There are various reasons that the show is
famous in Pakistan and other parts of the world. Let’s see what these reasons are. Family-Friendly Content One of the best reasons why people like this
Tv series is that it is family-friendly. There is no nudity, no adult stuff in whole
tv series. This is why people prefer it. It is best to watch for young adults and the
adult generation. There are some violence or battle scenes however
the young adults can watch these scenes easily. Another reason for its success is that the
show promotes Islam and its principle. There is constant preaching of Allah and also
the series contains no use of alcohol and drugs which promotes the same values in the
young generation as well. The young children who watch such series follow
the path of Islam and follow all its principles. Portrayal Another reason for the show's success is the
portrayal of the history of various nations one gets to see due to this series. The year of 2001 has left a deep scar on the
entire world and has led many people to create an image inside their minds of the Islamic
countries. These images make them believe that the Islamic
nation has a constant history of violence or bloodshed. However, when such people see series such
as Dirilis Ertugrul, they see an entirely different perspective of the history of the
Islamic nation. The image inside their head starts to change
due to such tv series. Lockdown Probably the common reason which people will
give you about the show’s popularity is the lockdown. When the lockdown occurred, people were locked
inside their homes for days and even months. During this time of having nothing to do,
people start to watch tv shows and online series. They finished many series and the one which
they like, they shared it amongst their friends. In this way, the popularity of the Dirilis
Ertugrul began. People watched the series once, loved it,
and then they recommended their other friends watch it too. The other friends as well watched the series,
they loved it and they too decided to see and recommend the series. This forms a chain and it led to the popularity
of this tv series all over the world. Availability of the Tv Series Dirilis Ertugrul has 5 seasons and each season
has a different number of episodes. People can watch Dirilis Ertugrul on their
tv sets as the series is streaming on TV in some Islamic countries. If you do not live in an Islamic country,
then you can watch this series on Netflix.

title: Omar
transcript_text: اين ابو بكر اين عثمان اين ابو عبيده اين عبد الرحمن بن عوف وما عرفنا بعد الا من كان له قوم يمنعونه فلم يابه ان يظهر دين محمد وهؤلاء من ذوي العقول والاحلام والمنازل او هكذا كنا نحسبهم فكيف نامن بمحمد على خدمنا وعبيدنا وصغار العقول والاحلام يا ابا الوليد او قال محمد قولته ثم اعتزلنا كما فعل من قبل ورقه ابن نوفل وزيد بن عمرو بن نفيل اللهان الامر وقلنا شانه وما يريد ولكنه يزعم انه نبي رسول من عند الله مامور بتبليغ الرساله فمن اطاعه نجى من اطاعه نجى ومن خالفه هلك فمن دخل في طاعته خرج من طاعتنا لا ولات ما هي بقوله يقولها الرجل ثم يمضي الى حال سبيله او يرجع الينا بعدها بالراي والط اعه كما يخلع بتلك القولة اصنامنا حتى يخلع طاعتنا فان كثر الناس من حوله قلوا من حولنا حتى نصبح وليس لنا فيهم راي ولا تدبير ولا حكم فهل نتركه وما يدعو اليه حتى يستشري هذا الخطب قد تاخرت علينا بالشراب يا بلال ما كنت لابدا بثه قريش قبل شيوخها نحن والله احوج الى الشراب من شيخ الرقه عظامه وذهب ووفره لا يسمعنا احدهم امازلت تخاف اباك حتى يوضع في قبره كيف رضيت ان يسرعك غلام جبير بن مطعم ذلك اليوم وحشي انه اقوى مني يا سيدي ولو كانت الغلبه بالتمني لانقلبت احوال الناس صدقت ولا كنت الان مع الشيوخ تصول وتجول وتقضي في امر محمد فيطيعك القوم هل تتمنى لو كان ذلك يا بلال لو اطلع الناس على امان الناس لما تصافحوا الا بالسيوف ولكن ما تصنع الجوارح وقد ملكتموها مني يا سيدي [موسيقى] فلكم علي ما على العبد من حلب الشياه وراعي الابل وحمل الابتلاء والوقوف في ناديكم لو كنت تحسن من اللعب بالحربه ما تحسنه من تلك اذا لتم لنا حظنا منك ليس بالطبع الحياه تمام الحظوظ يا سيدي والا استغنى الناس عن الناس ما علمت قبل اليوم ان له حكمه كنا احوج الى حربته وقوته [موسيقى] عبد ورائي اعظم خطرا من عبد وحربه [موسيقى] ذكرتم اشياء وغربت عنكم اشياء ذكرتم قريشا وغفلتم عن سائر العرب وقد علمتم ان كثيرا منهم قد نفسوا عليكم وانتم فيه من العز والمال والمنعه ومن غادوا اليكم الا بسلطان الدين الذي انتم وهم عليه وبحرمه الكعبه التي تختصون بجوارها وحفظها وسدانتها وان محمدا يزعم انه بعث للناس جميعا الا انه قد بدا بقومه وعشيرته فما هي حتى يتعرض لقبائل العرب فربما وجد بعضهم في ذلك سبيلا الى نبذكم فاغراهم باتباع محمد يغالبونكم ببعض رجالكم وتلكم الله يسوع ولا عمري لماذا يختار الله محمدا من بيننا ليكون نبيه ورسوله وفينا من هو اعظم منه مالا نعم نعم يا ابا الحكم ان كان محمد كاذبا فما كان ليوثر بدعوى النبوه غير نفسه وان كان صادقا فان الله يختار من يشاء ولا يستشير في حكمه احدا حيرتنا يا ابا الوليد هل تريدنا اذا ان نترك محمدا وشانا [موسيقى] قد علمتم يا معشر قريش انا بني هاشم لن يسلموكم صاحبهم وان خالفوا دينه واقاموا على الدينكم الا ان تقاتلوهم ويقاتلوكم نفعل والله اذا نقع فينا نفر منه تقطيع الارحام وانحلال عقد قريش واختلال احوالهم وطمع العرب فيهم فلم يقل قائلكم لو كانت كلمه يقولها الرجل ثم يرجع فيما عدا ذلك الى مالوف عيشنا وانتظام امرنا لهان علينا اذا [موسيقى] فانما نخشاه حقا من امر محمد عواقبه التي ذكرنا فهل نكون نحن اول من يسعى الى ذلك اذ نحتكم الى السيف [موسيقى] فما راي الراي ان تراجع فيه عمه وابا طالب فما عرفنا رجلا احب لقومه وابر به منه واحرص على اجتماع امرهم منه [موسيقى] وهو اجدر بان ينهى محمدا ويكفه فان فعل فقد كفاكم ما انتم فيه [موسيقى] كيف سولت لك نفسك تخالف رايي امام وجوه عبد مناف وانا خالك الم نجتمع اليودلي كل برايه ثم نجمع امرنا على ايها اقوى سبيلا وادنى ان يحقق الغايه ما زلت على ان جعلت رايك اقوى من راي خالك هذا بر كبير ما ذاك مقام البر بك ونحن جميعا نطلب لغايه نفسها وتختلف الوسائل وتصغر وجهي امام عتبه وشيوخ عبد مناف لم نجتمع ليقهر بعضنا بعضا بالراي انما اجتمعنا ننظر فيما يصلح امر قريش كلها وما كنت لاكتم رايا ارى فيه صلاح القوم وان وافق عبد مناف وخالف اخوالي وعلى هذا خالفت محمدا لا لخلق كرهته منه ولا لاحنه احمله عليه فوالله ما يغمز به احد الا عرف الناس كذبه ولا اخالفه لحسد احمله على بني هاشم فوالله لو كان من عدي ما اختلف الحال عندي قيد قنبله لان كان هذا رايك فما هو راي عتبه ابن ربيعه وسائر عبد شمس وعبد مناف الم ترى كيف كان يهون الخطب ويهدئ النفوس وما ذاك الا لانه ذكر ارحامه مع بني هاشم اذ هم جميعا لعبد مناف وقد يكون اقرب الناس رحما اشدهم تنافسا على السياده كل يرى نفسه عدلا لابن عمه فلا يرضى ان يتقدمه وما زال هذا حلب بني عبد شمس وبني هاشم فاذا تقدم بنو مخزوم نسم ما بينهم وذكروا انهم جميعا عبده وناف ولقد والله تنازعنا وبني عبد مناف الشرف اعطوا فاعطيناه واطعموا فاطعمنا وحملوا فحملنا حتى اذا تساوينا وكنا كفر سيران قالوا منا نبي ياتيه الوحي من السماء فمتى متى ندرك نحن بني مخزوم مثل هذه والله لا نؤمن به ولا نصدقه ولو جاءنا بكل ايه وان ابى بن هاشم الا منعاه وذبنا بهم ولم نبالي احفظ هذا عن خالك يا ابن حنطمه [موسيقى] لقد رايتك تخفي القوم وتخفف من غلواء ابي الحكم والتي ما غاضهم من محمد ما غضن منه ولكنه الحسد من عبد مناف هاشمها وعبد شمسها وان في الرجل حده وسفاهه على ما فيه من فطنه وقوه ولا 9 نعلم محمد عندي خير منه لولا ان محمدا قد فارق دينانا وسفها احلامنا وما الذي ينفسنا عليه ابو الحكم ان كانت شرا كما يرى ونرى فان محمدا قد جمعنا والناس على صعيد واحد وبدا بعشيرته الاقرب في الاقرب ان كان title: Kingdoms of Fire
transcript_text: [Music] the series kingdoms of fire is about life in arab lands under ottoman rule nearly 500 years ago according to a saudi press release the series claims to expose the tyranny of the ottomans attached to the film is british director peter webber whose resume includes girl with the pearl earring and hannibal rising so his kingdoms of fire and honest television series or seldi propaganda let's talk to miriam berg assistant professor in residence at northwestern university in qatar miriam your research focuses on influences of turkish media in foreign markets and turkey's growing role in international politics how effective are turkish tv series as soft power tools okay so turkish drama cereals have been uh significantly contributing over the years to the rise of turkey's soft power even though you know we can say the drama sector's finances have been far from soft representing a major economic trade interests with important repercussions to tourism for tourism so turkish cereals are an important catalyst for an increased awareness of turkey in the region which also prompts many viewers and their families to visit the country to experience what they see um on on the screen for themselves so in the past 10 years turkish series have become a permanent fixture on arab screens the overwhelming success in the middle east and north africa has not only aided in reconnecting with these former ottoman territories not known for the frontness fortman rule but has also contributed to turkish popular culture which is typically seen by these nationals of these countries as exciting rich influential trends setting and an example for a modern country that is achievable in a muslim arab context so turkish syria's significantly aided in bringing an entire country and its uh culture closer through commonalities that many in the arab world were unaware of and if i could add another point is that one of the core aspects and one of the core appeal i would say of turkish drama series to arab audiences and therefore its greatest soft power is its ability to touch up on subjects considered taboo in the arab world so turkish drama series project turkey as a nation where people have a choice you know they can be either liberal or conservative and they can live their lives that way but a society where family values and respect for one's older still matter so by doing so they fill a void that arab media fails to satisfy so turkish syria's aid in creating really an affinity a familiarity with turkey and its culture among arabs that no other public diplomacy incentive could possibly have achieved so then how did audiences react when the arab world's largest tv broadcaster nbc dropped turkish soap operas that's a good question so i just would like to add to that that this was done uh without prior note notice in march 2013 uh the arab world's largest private broadcaster as you just mentioned mbc group pulled the plug on turkish culture products and ironically the network that first ignited the fever for turkish cereals has been the one to now seemingly extinguish its existence in arab screen so with the shows no longer airing on saudi arabian or uae networks loyal viewers have been constrained to watching turkish serials on alternative platforms um findings from my own research an online survey that i administered to several arab fan sites of turkish serials um which included a sample of 300 respondents whom the majority were women between the ages of 18 to 50. um you know it really showed that loyal viewers are now opting to watch turkish serials either online with english or arabic subtitles through platforms like youtube or other blogs or they are visiting turkish channel websites and watch the show's original language and interestingly lots of people are actually learning turkish that way but another thing is that the younger generation we could say the 18 to 25 year olds have been now watching shows on netflix but another interesting maybe concerning thing is that the older generation maybe people above 50 what the data has shown is that they either stop watching or they're basically going to other satellite channels where these shows are still aired but dubbed in arabic but ultimately what these findings have revealed is that arab audiences are active they seek in control over content that best serves their needs irrespective of restrictions and censorship and post on them so we can all agree that in the mena region television is still maybe one of the most prominent uh you know ways to watch content but the new development shows that arab audiences now with the new media landscape freely engage with content they would like to engage rather than being forced to consume what state or media corporations wants them to consume so kingdoms of fire paints a picture that the glories of the ottoman empire were not so glorious after all especially crimes committed against egyptians and the ottomans theft of egyptian wealth what do you think of this sure nbc's decision to produce the serious kingdom of fire clearly shows that they believe that turkish cultural products cereals have an enormous impact on arab audiences so ottoman period dramas like magnificent century or resurrection air to rule have been hugely successful in the mena region they achieved what turkish foreign policy has sought to accomplish basically a renewed reconnection with former ottoman territories through rekindling of turkish nostalgia for an age of of the empire among countries that are not known for the fondness for ottoman rule so turkish culture projects have been uh a key in transforming a deep-rooted negative image of turkey and its ottoman past into more positive one among arab nationals so in my um research over the years i found that turkey's imperial past was increasingly seen as a period of arab rather than seen as a period of arab oppression turkish oppression was seen as a time where muslims collectively mattered now by creating a show emphasizing an ottoman cruelty they are trying to bring back the age-old arab narrative of the terrible turk and what is interesting here is that there is assume that the arab audience is passive and get influenced by anything they see on television and turkish shows are successful because they are well produced have talented actors touch on stories that are not touched on by art media so by again producing a show uh that's kind of where the producers think it will resonate with the audience doesn't mean it will resonate with the audience and it will be successful and achieve what it's trying to achieve thank you very much for your time it will be very interesting to see how this all plays out [Music] you

100 Venetian ducas, Isabel. And 50 Spanish reales from me. One of the 100 shares
of the Basca for you. And half a share for you. You can claim your share
once the goods arrive and are sold. Don't you know how much
a real is worth, Isabel? What's half a share? Its worth varies.
Different here as well. We get to decide what money
is worth what around here. Wheat from the villages
near the shore. Got 'em on the harbour. -How many sacks?
-So many. I'm not smart enough to count all. Good. It'll be worth
much during famine. I'll make the calculations
and strike a bargain. You just bring the men here. We got valuable goods, Isabel. We want the guard duties
from men of your choosing... ...before we depart from Alexandria. I will assign
my most trusted guards to you. They should be back in a day or two.
You can leave at once then. You got a deal, Isabel. Your most trusted guards you say? I was your most trusted once. Everyone makes mistakes. Yes. It was a mistake too.
Don't I deserve forgiveness? I don't mean you, Cafer. It was my mistake to trust you. I still can't forgive
myself at times. Direna! Tell the cook
that I want a good tajin of lamb... ...with plums and almonds.
Oruç will be back soon. They must have missed a good feast. I'm pregnant, right? Come on, tell me, Asiye Teyze. I'm pregnant,
I'm not making it up, right? I'm Asiye of many years
and you're pregnant, Despina. -Thank God!
-With a son, in fact. May God bless you, Asiye Teyze! You gave the world to me! You'll give Baba Oruç good news
when he is back. I better tidy up. What is it? Won't you tell him? Let's wait a few more weeks.
Just to be sure. Allah Allah. Well, we tried before but
God tested us every time... ...and didn't grant us one. I wouldn't want to get
Oruç's hopes up for nothing. I think you should tell him. Seamen are hard to domesticate.
They may wander about. Even if this is wrong news
it will keep his mind on you. Don't you worry. Don't tarnish my house with other
people's lies. One word of Oruç was enough for me
to leave behind my father's home. If he up and leaves me,
I'll say that he wasn't mine. Say that it was a dream
and then leave.

title: Yunus Emre: Journey of Love
transcript_text:
THE JOURNEY OF LOVE -Peace be upon you
-And upon you. You seem to have lost sleep because of your excitement. I saw it in my dream last night. You passed the approval exam and became a qadi in a big city. They say that dreams mean the opposite. If you're afraid of Siraceddin Hazrat, I'll sit the approval exam
for you in disguise. Ah, well, then. Do the prayer
for me in disguise, too. Let's see if you
will be this witty before the exam committee. Come on, then. After you. Lord, grant this humble
servant of Yours success in today's exam
and all future exams. This is your final exam in
Karatay Madrasa, Yunus. Your long years of training, where you've been taught by Hodja Rukneddin Mazderani, our Master Semsettin
Mardini, and humbly me has come to
an end as of today. As a matter of fact,
it is knowledge itself that determines
test results. It is not up to these
teachers of yours, my humble self included,
to evaluate the results. Deciding the outcome
lies with knowledge itself. This knowledge is such a Sirat with heaven on one side
and hell on the other. One side is the future, word,
position, and on the other side
one is all by oneself. Yunus, you know the
great books of hadith, the Kutub al-Sittah. Now, tell us, of these six hadith books, which one are we
to rely on the most? You know the answer, sir. Tell us the answer,
 so that we know it. We memorise the Noble Quran
and Sahih al-Bukhari lest a huge fire should
burn down the whole world, all creatures and humankind alike. The ashes of which shall turn into a pair of wings
and fly us away. If Al-Bukhari is enough, then why do we need Muslim? Burn his work away.
What do you say, sir? If one of the six is gone,
five will remain. If we burn the second one,
the rest is useless. What do you think
about it, Yunus? What has been agreed upon is the best of a bad bunch, sir. What do you mean by 'agreed upon'? It's the alliance of opposing parties that will give us the best
gift of judgment, sir. If the alliance between Bukhari and Muslim provides
the best results, why do we need the rest of them? Hadith studies is
a six-walled room, sir. Each one of the Kutub al-Sittah
represents a wall. Does it make any difference if one of the walls
is high or short? Both the short and the long one
make up the whole and complete it. Quite a poet, are you, Yunus? Not at all, sir. I learned
rhetoric from you. Poets tell lies. We should not merely talk, we must be committed to working for and serving Muslims. Everybody's talking
about how you amazed even Siraceddin
Hazrat in the exam. Well, you'll be appointed
qadi in a big city. Then you'll marry a beautiful woman and forget about us. Is it what you want for
yourself or for me, Ilyas? So what? You mean to remain a bachelor
throughout your life? Surely there's a beautiful girl
you keep in your heart. You're talking as if you just met me today. This is not the day we met, but the day we
bid farewell, Ilyas. You’re right. And you? What do you think? Guess you'll marry Gokce soon. Let's see what the morning holds. We'll see where we
are to be appointed to. God protect us. The Mongols have destroyed Kirsehir. They say that Ahi Evran and all his supporters
were slaughtered. There's fire all over the Seljuk Empire. I say, let's see where
we will be appointed to. We should not wish for a
big city for ourselves, Ilyas, But for the sake of struggle and effort. If this people had stuck to the rules of
religion and justice, could the Mongols have attacked us? Whenever we ignored justice, didn't we find
ourselves in trouble? Where's the cure? At lodges where they sing and dhikr? Or those uneducated, self-appointed lodge sheikhs? Or illiterate dervishes? Madrasah is the cure, Ilyas. If there's any cure
other than madrasah, they're more than welcome! If a word has no purpose, it's nothing but nonsense. There is no time to waste
on empty talk, sons. We know that justice is the basis of
unity and strength. Justice is the pole
that keeps the sky above us and the
ground underneath. Woe if it comes down. Then, it'll be worse
than doomsday. Sons, from this day
on, each one of you will represent Hazrat Omar. Bear in mind that
the real authority of your office is Hazrat Omar. Had it not been for Omar, man would have never
known about justice. You, new qadi candidates, do keep this in your mind. Wherever you happen to be employed in the Seljuk Empire, never stray from the path of science, justice, and sharia! Our duty is not to do politics but to serve justice. Ilyas, son of Mubariz. Qadi of Elmali. Serve justice at all times. Ertokus, son of Rustem. Qadi of Divrigi. Serve justice at all times. Yunus, son of Abdullah. Qadi of Nallihan. Serve justice at all times. Izzettin, son of Mehmet. Dear God, grant me
success on this path. Whoa! You're thirsty, too. Hold on for a bit longer. Surely, we will find a river. Come now. Old man! -Peace be upon you
-And upon you. Where do you come from,
where are you going? I am on a never ending journey. That is for sure, though. Well, tell me then,
my horse is dying of thirst. Is there a water
source hereabouts? It’s likely to rain
cats and dogs soon. Are you kidding me, old man? Talking is free. So is silence, son. I've asked you a question. I asked if there's a
water source nearby, but you don't care to answer. To mock me further, you even said that
it's going to rain. God's grace will come down soon. If we depend on the
rain in such weather, we'll definitely die of thirst. The weather was clear. How did you know it would rain? It's my knees, son. Whenever it rains
my knees hurt. I thought you worked miracles. Can there be a better
miracle than this? How is this a miracle? Is it you who
makes it rain? Of course not! It's not, but it's the rain that gives me this pain in my knees. Do you believe in such a superstition? Not in superstition,
I believe in God, son. Whatever we are blessed with
in this life, it is from God. And there is nothing beyond God. That stone, those
trees, birds, wolves, everything has a form. And human beings
are in the human form. Now, if you have a headache, your brain knows
that it's a headache. If you step on a thorn, would your foot recognise
the pain or your brain? So, man is a fragment of this realm. Yes. We are the dust of the same flower. Therefore, the rain hurts my knees before it begins. So, if it's not the real
miracle, what is it, son? That was quite impressive, old man. Such an accurate description
without scientific truths! But our path is the Islamic
jurisprudence, hadith, tafsir. Thus, sharia is possible by gaining knowledge
about every science. Don’t take it personally,
but if those words were said by someone unwary, it would be blasphemy. If one knows God, one
can't be blasphemous. The rain has slowed down. We need to get to
an inn before sunset. Godspeed! Safe journey, son! Don't you know that thieves and bandits are
everywhere, old man? You can't go on alone. I'm a poor old man. I have no money
nor golden teeth. If bandits see me, they’ll just have mercy on me. Have a good trip. If I told you that the man you
entered the cave with to take shelter from the rain is the new qadi of Nallihan,
what would you say? I'd say I entered the cave not to take shelter
from the rain... but to do the afternoon prayer. Have a safe journey. -Welcome, sir.
-Whoa! -Thank you.
-You go in, sir. Eat something. It seems you have
long been travelling. Yes. It’s a little sweaty,
don’t keep it in the wind. Don’t worry. This way, sir. Welcome, sir.
Come take a seat. -Peace be upon you.
-And upon you. -What can I get you?
-Bulgur with meat. Very well, sir. May I sit here? Do you always take action first and ask for permission later? The water comes from Gokluk, sir.
It's healing. -Would you like anything else?
-Thank you. Ah, bulgur with meat looks yummy. I want the same, innkeeper. Yes, sir. What do you do? I should be asking that question. You sat at my table
without permission and are calling me to account? Tell me, what do you do? Pretty much anything. Any job for me? Depends on what you do. Innkeeper, refresh! -Here you are, sir.
-May I have the bill? One akche will
be more than enough, sir. Thank you. Innkeeper! I'll show you your room, sir.
Please. Shh! If you scream, you die. Did you see the thief? Stop! Hey! What is it, sir? A man robbing a qadi of this country would readily kill its people. But sooner or later,
I'll find that thief. I'll find him and bring him to justice! Forgive me, Qadi Efendi.
Forgive me! It's not your fault. We are helpless, Qadi Efendi! Those thieves risk our lives
every single day here. An eye for an eye,
a tooth for a tooth. You now have a Seljuk
Qadi here in Nallihan! There will be justice!
There will be sharia! Unless my eyes go blind,
I will recognise that face. I'll find him
and make him pay for it! God bless you,
Qadi Effendi. Look after my books.
I'll send a man to get them. Yes, sir. Old man! -Peace be upon you.
-And upon you. We meet again. Well, this is it. Take to the road,
you'll reach your destination. Stray from it, you won't. Won't you ask me
what's become of me? Such as where my horse is
and why I'm on foot. Does anyone need a reason for walking? I'm not used to walking
as much as you, old man. Does the road know who's on it? On horseback or on foot? Where there is no justice, bandits, thieves, and murderers
are the qadi. You might say that we have mayors and qadis
in this country. Meritocracy has to replace nepotism. If an uneducated person who
has never been to a madrasah attains a position
which he doesn't merit, the result will be this! I am a poor old man, Qadi Efendi. How should I know? I'm illiterate, I did not go
to school or madrasah. You didn't go to school or madrasah but none of those
so-called educated ones would be able to speak as efficiently as
you do, old man. I don't know. Not you, old man. From now on, let those
thieves, bandits fear! Recite the iqama, old man. I've seen such things on this road before I become qadi I don't know
whether to laugh or cry. All kinds of people who strayed from the path of God. A thief at the inn
stole everything I had. Including my appointment notice,
inkstand, even my inkwell. What would a thief
do with inkwell and inkstand if he's not
on the path of God? I don't think this is something
to joke about! A thief is a thief, and an ominous
person is ominous. I'm not a dervish! Being so naive is
another form of oppression. How would a servant know who
is the victimiser or victim? If you ask any path to me, it is one
and it leads to God. Hence, all creations of God
walk on the path to God. Would one find God by stealing from and
oppressing the innocent? Shall I tell you the
story of Ibrahim Ethem who was the Sultan of hearts
and of the city of Rey? If you'd like to hear it. If you wish to tell me,
then I'd like to listen. Before walking to God, abandoning his throne and crown, Ibrahim Ethem would sit on his throne and think day and night. "O Lord, open my heart's eye," he would pray. But he couldn't find a cure. One day he fell asleep on his throne. His eye was asleep,
but he was awake inside. He heard footsteps on the roof. Then the footsteps
of dozens of people filled the privy chamber of the palace. Sultan Ibrahim Ethem was baffled. He didn't even have the strength to call to his guards. "What's this noise? Who is running on
the roof of my palace?". Then he saw a man
leaning out of the window. "Who are you?" he asked him. And the man answered. "I'm from so-and-so village." "Very well, but what are you doing upon the roof of my palace?" The man leaning out of
the window answered. "I'm looking for my lost cows." Ibrahim Ethem was surprised. "Are you crazy? Why look
for them on the roof?" "You are the crazy one
here," the man snapped. "You're searching
for God on a golden throne and believe you're sane, but you think I'm crazy for I'm looking for my
lost cows on the roof?" Nice story, old man. A nice story for one
to learn a lesson from. A wise saying for
one to be inspired by. By putting it out,
are you intending to leave us to the
mercy of wolves? What if this 100-year-old tree catches fire
because we're afraid of the wolves and
keep it burning? Hey! Hey, you! Hey, stop! Stop, don't run away! Stop! Stop! Don't be afraid, there. Murderer! Murderer! A man has killed
someone over there! I saw the murderer, but I missed him. Amen. He ran away
before I crossed the river. Don’t worry, Qadi Efendi. He may have escaped from you but he won't escape his fate. But I saw his face,
and I'll never forget it. If the person you
saw is the murderer, fate will find him. I said I saw him
with my own eyes! What if you had my failing eyes? When I'm praying,
I can't really tell it's rock or soil
I'm praying on. Are you with the qadi or
with the murderer, old man? Neither with the qadi
nor with the murderer, son. I am with the truth. If he's not the murderer,
why did he escape? Do you think you might
have scared him away? It is my duty! Your duty is to serve as a qadi. Perhaps his duty is to escape. You speak well.
Perfect rhetoric. And if you were well-educated, you would be
Chief Mudarris in Karatay. We part here, Qadi Efendi. So this time we
part for good, eh? Even if we part,
the path is one. Indeed! I've experienced it. I'll find that murderer as soon as I take office. Time to bid farewell then. God bless you. God grant you
success in your duty. Amen. During the reign
of Sultan Izzettin the Second there was a Greek priest here. The priest would tell the community "Your sheikh is an impostor. "Have you ever seen him
work miracles?" One day, he gathered the community and went to the dervish lodge. When they appeared before the sheikh, he was holding the wooden sword
of Demirci Bayram. Well, Demirci Bayram
was just a boy at that time. The community was baffled. Our sheikh
was fighting with invisible enemies. The priest stood up and said, "That's your sheikh!" "Obviously, he is not
quite right in the head. If we ask him, he'll say that
he's fighting heathens and you'll believe that." Do you know what our sheikh did? He took off his robe. His sacred body
was covered in blood. Subhanallah. At the time, Sari Saltuk Hazrat
was in a war in Bulgaria. An army of heathens
was attacking our fighters. The fighters were desperate. But, of course, our
sheikh presaged it. How about the priest?
What did he do when he saw this? Why, he recited
the shahadah there. If you don't believe me, ask Doctor Mikel. That priest was Doctor Mikel's flesh and blood uncle. Our sheikh is coming! Bismillahirrahmanirrahm. Bismillahirrahmanirrahm. -Peace be upon you.
-And upon you. Welcome, sheikh. Welcome, sheikh. Welcome, sir. Thank you, wife. Please, no. We've been waiting
for you for so long. I brought you greetings from
Sister Fatma. And to all the dervishes here. How was your journey?
Did you have company? Yes, I did. I had a very good companion. Bismillahirrahmanirrahim. Bismillahirrahmanirrahim. All praise is for God,
Lord of all worlds. All praise is for God,
Lord of all worlds. the Most Compassionate,
Most Merciful. the Most Compassionate,
Most Merciful. Hey, people! Hear ye! Hear ye! Emir Rukneddin... wishes the caravans to
set off for Yabanli Bazaar. Hear ye! Hear ye! Emir Rukneddin wishes the caravans to
set off for Yabanli Bazaar. As if there are any goods
to take to Yabanli Bazaar! Tell me about it, Bayram Aga. We're already burdened by taxes. No goods for the bazaar. Hear ye! Hear ye! The Mongol Commander Baycu
Noyan has completely destroyed Sivas. Baba Sinan Khalifa of Ahi Evran and all his followers including children
have been put to the sword. Hear ye! Hear ye! -Many thanks.
-May it heal you. What's done is done!
The dead are dead! What's done is done!
The dead are dead! What's done is done!
The dead are dead! What's done is done!
The dead are dead! What's done is done!
The dead are dead! What's done is done!
The dead are dead! We're here, Qadi Efendi.
Your chamber is upstairs. Before you, Qadi Himmet Effendi
of Burdur resided here. Thank you, Hasan Aga. After the morning prayer, you all should be
ready in the hall. Very well, Qadi Efendi. Please, just light the candles. We've prepared
everything in your room. If you need anything else,
just ask for it. -Thank you.
-Good night. -Peace be upon you.
-And upon you. It's our duty and only aim to balance the scales of justice. Well, how can we
keep the scales balanced? Whoever commits fraud or confiscates one's property or makes an
attempt on one's life must know that he'll
be brought to justice. Let everyone know that if I, Qadi Yunus,
commit a crime, the law will be applied. Let kids who play in the
streets of Nallihan know that the source of law
is not the qadi but sharia! -Subashi Hasan Aga.
-Yes, Qadi Efendi? Has the Ahi Organisation been informed? Yes, it has, Qadi Effendi. They are ready whenever you wish. Good. Take four officers with you. Let us see what is happening in the streets of Nallihan.

bloody ditches and collaborated with... ...Crusaders and all other infidels

her foot out of this room ever again. Understood? Never again! Do you understand me clearly, Nasir? If you ever fail again... ...I will kill and
hang your body at El Aziz's balcony. I would do this. I swear! How do you know that
Aftalia is in the tribe? They know that they cannot
even breathe in Aleppo. The tribe is their only refuge. If Aftalia speaks, Aleppo would fall.
We can never allow that. Send one of your best men to the tribe. Aftalia must die. -I am going, Dogan.
-Bamsi. How much longer are we going to sit? Are we going to sit and get fat like
a breeding stock? You mountain bear, what did our Bey say?
Didn't he tell us to wait? Turgut is not with us,
Ertugrul Bey is not with us. How can we go back to the tribe? How are we going to look

I shall send our armies, Your Highness. I know that my uncle is there as well,
hiding amongst the Kayis. Do you think the soldiers would attack

in our hands, Your Highness. The Seljuk Shahzada and his daughter. If you wed the Shahzada's daughter... ...no one can stand against your power. Then, Seljuk intrusions at our borders and
the recklessness of the Templars would end. You should welcome the opportunity,
Your Highness. Amanos Mountains
Templars Castle You were right, sir. They managed to infiltrate even here. The newcomers. I want both of them, Marcus. Dead or alive. Leila... Governess, you betrayed Leila
whom you raised since she was a baby. I did what was right. For you and your brother. We will all pay for your mistake.
Now, please go away. I don't want to see you. Permission? My Shahzada, the Ameer is expecting you.
Your daughter as well. I will put you in a special room
for your safety, my Shahzada. Who are you protecting us from,
Your Highness? From traitor Templars
and their collaborators. What about Ertugrul? You witnessed what he did
in my palace as well, Halime Sultan. Nothing is as it is seen, Your Highness. Is there anything
you want to talk with me, Your Highness? If you give permission, yes. I am listening. Against this evil pact... ...against my emirate and
the whole Muslim world... ...I plan to start a big war. That is why I request your help. I would do all I can, Your Highness. In order to show our power to the enemy... ...with your permission, my Shahzada... ...I wish to marry your daughter.

a wild man for the first time. Be patient. When Melik Saladin
gets permission from the Sultan... ...we'll go back. Then you'll fight as you please
at the borders and feel relieved. There he is. The decision will be made at the
council meeting. We have little time. We have to go to Ascalon before
Gabriel and the Templar's attack. The meeting will probably finish
at night, Sir Melik. I haven't seen my son Ömer
for a long time. I'd like to visit my family home
and catch up with them. Scholar Ömer? He's probably at his desk. Since we'll wait,
let's wait with Ömer. Plus, I'll see
my best friend's precious. You shouldn't bother, Sir Melik. I'll bring him to kiss your hand
at a convenience. No, you can't have a scholar
come to you. Besides, I'm like an uncle to him. Seeing my nephew won't bother me. -Then, let's go, Sir Melik.
-Come on. DAMASCUS - WOMEN'S BAZAAR
They're great.
Have them make 10 more. This won't do. Make the spoons out of birch,
so that they're sturdy. Yes, Fatma Khatun. Zümrüt Khatun,
good luck with the work. Thank you, Fatma Khatun. This is a nice engraving
on the dagger. Who ordered this? I'm making it for Şirkuh. The one
he has on his waist is worn. I wanted the new one to be
worthy of an army commander. Don't you think it's better for you
to make personal stuff at home? He's my man. What's wrong with that? Besides, don't worry. The girls
will take care of the work here. Fatma Khatun. I have good news.
-What gives? Your son Şehinşah's here. I saw him going home. Şehinşah. "Read: In the name of Allah
Who created." What is meant here by "read" Ömer? To read the Holy Quran
and understand what it means. "Iqra" also means "to invite." -Father!
-My son. Ömer. It is to invite people to the beauty,
kindness, righteousness... ...unity that Allah orders. All sayings in the Quran
are full of meaning treasures. I knew that. I was about to say it
when you said it. Son. That Melik Saladin. You're passionate about
knowledge Ömer. Well done. Well done. Don't ever lose that. Okay? Nobody could beat you in the debates
in the madrasa. You haven't changed.
-Thank you, Müderris Efendi. I haven't been in a scholars debate
because I've been busy with my sword. I hope I'll stop by.
-We'll be honoured. Excuse me. Please, Sir Melik.
-Şehinşah! Mother. We've missed you so much. Son! Thank goodness. You all look well. Thank goodness. Welcome. I guess you're one of the
Border Veterans like Şehinşah. He's the leader of
the Border Veterans, father. Sultan Nureddin's son, Melik Saladin. Son. Mother. You didn't hug me like that.
I'll be jealous. Actually, I'll be jealous, Şehinşah. I have never seen my own mother. I've felt a longing for a mother
for years now. But you gave me such a hug... ...that I've felt a warmness
in my heart for the first time. I guess that's what
the warmth of a mother is like. Because... ...I am your... ...wet nurse. I'll give my child to you, whose
scent I haven't got enough of yet... ...but... ...I have one condition. Even if my son doesn't know
I'm his mother... ...I want to nurse him. It's his food in my breasts. Besides, he's so little. He'll get cold anywhere
other than his mother's arms. My poor baby won't get warm. What makes us brothers... ...is the milk we had
from the same mother, Şehinşah. If that's the case,
we haven't you told this before? Interesting,
none of us knew about it. Well, why are we all standing here? Come on, get some rest. Come on. Come on, please. Come on. DAMASCUS
It's in our state's benefit
to help Ascalon, My Sultan. If we save Ascalon... ...Ascalon people will pledge their
allegiance to us, not the Fatimid. That way,
we'll have the key to Jerusalem. You're the vizier of the state... ...but you still don't know
the Fatimid yet... ...Sir Vizier. Do you think the Fatimid
will give up on Ascalon easily? As if it's not enough for us
to fight the Crusaders... ...now we'll have to deal with
the Fatimid? You're unaware of the power
of the state you're the emir of... ...Emir Reyhani. We have the sword power to fight
both the Fatimid and the Crusaders. Besides, Melik Saladin's right. If Gabriel capture Ascalon
we'll be his first target. We have to put out the flames
before it causes a fire. What's the sword power
you're talking about, Şirkuh? A couple of Border Veterans
with Saladin? This isn't like raiding castles
on hills... ...or setting ambushes on the roads. Going to Ascalon means throwing
the state into the fire, My Sultan. But if we don't go, a bigger fire
will break out right next to us. What matters is
to be able to take a step... ...by foreseeing the future,
not by looking at now. Come in. My Sultan. King Baldwin of Jerusalem is here. Wow... Border Veteran Şehinşah. Welcome. Thank you. So when your family's out of sight,
it's out of mind as well, huh? You didn't say you were coming. We had to come suddenly, brother. You're never out of my mind. Well, I don't know, brother. You've been with Melik Saladin
for a long time. Well, his position is greater
than all. We thought you no longer see
those down when you're up high. Şehinşah is with me
not to be glorified... ...but to glorify. And I'm their comrade
in the holy war. So you... I mean, you Sir...
-Sultan Nureddin's son, Saladin. Forgive me, Sir Melik. I didn't know. There a lot of things
you don't know, Turanşah. What don't I know, Yenge? Melik Saladin is your milk brother. Swords. Sultan Nureddin is ready for you. I thought... ...you were trapped in Jerusalem,
King Baldwin. What brings you to Damascus? Your son, Saladin. What about my son? He came to Jerusalem. What was he doing in Jerusalem? He came as the messenger of
Sultan Nureddin. Didn't you know about it,
Sultan Nureddin? Plus, he attempted
an assassination on us. My son is my constant messenger. He goes anywhere he wants,
when he wants on my behalf. But he will never attempt
an assassination. That's slander. If we ever have a score to settle,
we settle it in the battlefield. Not with assassinations. Saladin killed all the knights
who went after the assassin. My soldiers saw it. I guess he was afraid
that the truth would come out. But still, he gave himself away. How do we know this isn't slander? I wouldn't have come here
over a slander. I'm here for you to understand
how serious this is. Ask your son if you want. Let's see if he denies what he did. Let's say that
he attempted the assassination. Well, why would the Melik
would attempt such a thing? His target was the commander
of the Templar's, Gabriel. He wanted to kill him
so that he wouldn't attack Ascalon. Melik Saladin did everything
without the government's knowledge. We'll do what needs to be done. This shouldn't harm the peace
between the two states. It's not that simple. Saladin is the melik of
the Zengid state. The Zengid state will pay the price
for it. What price are you talking about? You took over the duty
to protect the pilgrimage route. I know how important that is
for you Muslims. Just as that's important for you... ...Ascalon is just as important
for us. We'll capture Ascalon. If you attempt to intervene... ...I'll block the pilgrimage road
on all sides. Not one Muslim can pass through. Are you threatening me? It's not a threat. It's a deal. Don't touch us... ...and we won't touch you. If we see even one person
from the Zengid in Ascalon... ...the pilgrimage route
will be hell for Muslims. Then... ...what will you look like in the
eyes of Muslims and your Caliph? Think about that... ...Sultan Nureddin. ASCALON Oh, Ascalon. Were you supposed to be left alone
by those you pledged allegiance to... ...and those you considered friends? We've been here for 500 years. And we've been alone
for 500 years, brother. Those we considered friends
always left us alone. We defended it alone. And today,
we'll defend it alone, too. Sir Emir Mansur. Did you send forth
the lookouts around, Şahsuvar? The Templar's should be here soon. They should keep a sharp lookout.
-We sent forth the lookouts... ...we have a bigger problem. Süreyya Khatun. She went to Jerusalem
disguised as a merchant. When Gabriel, the leader
of the Templar's were there. Süreyya Khatun. Süreyya? Is it true what I heard? You--
-It's true, dad. I went to Jerusalem.
-Why? To kill that degenerate Gabriel. I disguised myself and attacked him. But he avoided my arrow of death
at the last minute. Honey, are you out of your mind?
How could you do such a thing? Why would you attempt to
assassinate him, huh? When I killed Gabriel... ...the Templar's would've been left
without a leader been in chaos. And we would've taken advantage
of that to put an end to them. We wouldn't have given them
an opportunity to attack Ascalon. Your life matters, too,
Süreyya Khatun. You have to kiss that girl
on the forehead, brother. She did what many men couldn't do
for your homeland. An Ascalon woman,
worthy of our blood. What are you talking about, Esma? This isn't the right move to make
when the Templar's are coming for us. It'll be worse now. How can it be
worse than it is now, father? Look. Even the Fatimid, to which we
pledged allegiance, can't send help. Nobody hears us. It's like the whole world
is blind and deaf to us. That's the best I could do. And that's what I did
for our homeland. -Father!
-Halil? What's the rush? There are horsemen approaching
the city wall. Who are they? Gabriel. They're the lookouts we send forth. They took two more lives. I hope they end up in hell. Everybody, get ready. Gabriel waged the war. DAMASCUS Yes, My Sultan. How could you
go to Jerusalem, Saladin? How could you get involved
in an assassination? My Sultan, I have nothing to do
with the assassination. I told that to King Baldwin as well.
-King Baldwin came all the way here. He mouthed off to us. And because of you,
we couldn't find anything to say. You killed the knights
who went after the assassin. Is that true?
-Yes. I wanted to find out the reason
of the attempted assassination. But as I said My Sultan,
I didn't plan the assassination. I know that you didn't plan it But of course you'd be framed for it
after all you did. How could you not
think of that, Saladin? What happened, happened. You'll forget about Ascalon. You won't go to help Ascalon. How's that possible, My Sultan? We'll just leave it to the Crusaders? If we help Ascalon... ...King Baldwin said
he'd intercept the pilgrimage route. He's obviously trying to force
your hand by blackmailing you. We can't risk such danger
for Ascalon people. We can't play
with the lives of many Muslims. If Gabriel captures Ascalon... ...won't the lives of the Muslims
in our lands be in danger? Isn't Ascalon the only Muslim soil
near Jerusalem, father? What about our goal of Jerusalem? Our cause of uniting the Islam world.
Aren't they important? If you hadn't gone to Jerusalem
none of this would've happened. For you to get involved in
that assassination tied our hands. We agreed with you... ...but like Our Sultan said... ...we can't risk the pilgrimage route
over Ascalon. This is what the greater interests
of the state requires, Sir Melik. This is my final decision. The council meeting is over. Why did you tell Saladin
that you were his wet nurse, Fatma? I couldn't help myself, Ayyub. Years ago,
I gave my son away personally... ...and when I saw
him in front of me... ...I let go of myself. Sultan Nureddin trusted us... ...and let us in on this secret. No matter what happened
you had to be astute. Give me a break, Ayyub. Don't you know? My heart's been bleeding for years. Do you think I'm different? But this is a secret. It's important for all of us. Let's hope that... ...Saladin wouldn't suspect
and look into it. Or none of us can stop what happens. May I, father? Come, Saladin. Why, dad? Why are you stopping me? You instilled this cause in me. You taught me
that Muslims should unite... ...and Jerusalem should be free of
Crusader rule as you brought me up. I don't care if the whole world
doesn't understand me. But I wish you understood me,
acknowledged me. The Ascalon matter aside... ...that's what hit me the most. I was like you when I was young. I thought I could take care
of everything, defeat everybody... ...with my courage and sword. Because I just had a life to lose. And I don't care if I lose it
over my cause. But then... ...fate put the burden of sultanate
on my shoulders. It placed me on the Throne of Nessus. And next thing I know,
there's a lot to lose. There's a state. The lives of the people. That's when I understood
I can't solve everything... ...with my sword or my courage. And I got you, son. I got you. But you didn't get me. But I won't resent you for that. Because I know... ...that you can only get me
when you replace me. What happens if I do, father? Ascalon will overtly be
captured by the Crusaders. We'll be giving our own lands
to the enemy on a platter. I can't accept to stand back. You'll have to learn that
you should accept some facts. I'm still learning as well. Well, do you share all the facts... ...with me, father? Of course, son. Of course. Then, why didn't you tell me
Fatma Khatun was my wet nurse? Why didn't you share it with me? She breastfed you for a short while. I didn't feel the need to tell you. I wish you did. I've suffered for not having a mother
for years. Whenever I saw a mother her child... ...my heart ached. Because not having a mother
aches your heart. If you told me
who my wet nurse was... ...I could've soothed that ache
with her. We'll do it as you say, My Sultan. After all,
you have a state and people. You're responsible for their welfare. But my responsibility is... ...to help the oppressed
to the best I can. To never let a child
lose their mother. I can't sit back and do nothing
in the face of tyranny. My father should know that. Every choice... ...has a price, Saladin. And the harshest
of those prices is... ...to disobey the state's decision. The moment you step into Ascalon... ...it'll mean that
you risked that price. -I wonder what the decision is.
-What can it be? I'm sure the Sultan give permission
for a battle. Just wait, you dirty infidels. Sir Melik. What happened Sir Melik? They didn't allow us
to go to Ascalon. How? If we go to Ascalon... ...King Baldwin said that he'd
intercept the pilgrimage route. They've tied the hands of wolves,
the dogs will hit the streets. They won't. We'll go to Ascalon
even if the army doesn't come. Forgive me, Sir Melik. But if you disobey
the state's decision... ...it won't end well. Ascalon's salvation depends on us,
our salvation depends on Ascalon. Glory will clear us of all charges. I risked my life
as I set out on this path. I won't back down even my father
makes me pay for it or the enemy. Are you with me?
-We are. I'm with you, too. Allow me to come with you, Sir Melik. Turanşah, it's better for you
to be here. Our journey might take us
to our deaths. Even so, isn't that
for a greater cause, Sir Melik? I've always wanted to serve
for a cause like this. There can't be a greater cause
than this. Şehinşah is my own brother. And you're my milk brother. I don't want to stand back when
my brothers are risking their lives. Ascalon. ASCALON
You'll be the place where
I'll revive my father's earldom. I'll make you the city
of my revenge and love. Sir, Saladin set out for Ascalon. I thought King Baldwin
tied Nureddin's hands. But obviously, his son Saladin
severed those ties. Surely, he knows I'll put them
through hell when I capture Ascalon. I expect nothing less
from someone like Saladin. The resistance in Ascalon will
be stronger... ...when Saladin shows up
with his men. Place men around Ascalon. They shouldn't let Saladin
in the city. Yes, Sir. By the time Saladin arrives... ...we will have captured Ascalon. The war's beginning! It doesn't look good. Ascalonians are trying to resist. But clearly, they're less in numbers. The siege's becoming tight. Let's raid the knights
attacking the city wall. That way, we'll break
one front of the siege. They have more men than us, Turanşah.
We can't risk casualties. The only thing we'll do is to enter
Ascalon and support the defense. As Ascalon's strength increases... ...the knights' strength
will decrease. All right, but, the place is
surrounded everywhere, Sir Melik. How will we enter Ascalon? Take the wounded to the hospital
immediately. Immediately. Go help them immediately! I can bear the loss of
even one Ascalonian. Approach cautiously.
Don't let the Templar's see you. We'll pass through here and enter
into Ascalon through the passageway. Come on. Hurry! Come on! We need someone here! I'm coming! Replace the wounded, Şahsuvar. We don't have men to replace,
Süreyya Khatun. We had tens of wounded and dead
in one attack. I don't know what'll happen
in the next one. What's the situation
on the city walls? The same. We keep getting casualties there. At this rate,
we'll have lost Ascalon by morning. -Come on! Bring it!
-Hurry! Run! Come on! Come on! Run! We barged in here... ...what if this is a trap
Ascalonians set up for the enemy? Oh, man. You'll die not in the battlefield,
but because of your delusions. Come on, hurry up. Don't move. Who are you? Why are you sneaking into Ascalon
like thieves? What's your intention? We're here to help. Who are you to help us? I'm Saladin,
Sultan Nureddin Zengi's son. Halil, you've placed the archers,
haven't you? -We have, father.
-Good. Take these as well. Hurry. What's going on here, Süreyya? They sneaked into Ascalon. They're saying that
they're here to help us, father. Who are they? He's Nureddin Zengi's son. My enemy's son... ...is here to help me, huh? Who do you think you're fooling? We're not fooling anybody. Ascalon isn't important
just for you. It's important both for us
and the whole Islam world. That's why we're here. Nureddin Zengi must be
up to something, I know it. Just because of your animosity
towards us... ...you've teamed up
with the Templar's, haven't you? And you'll hit us inside
under the guise of helping. Do we need to hit you inside? You're gradually perishing. If we allow them, the Templar's
will eventually destroy you. We're here, so that you don't perish,
get destroyed... ...so that Ascalon doesn't fall
into the hands of the Crusaders. What's Nureddin Zengi's intention
by helping us? Does he think we'll obey him
when Ascalon is saved? Is that his plan? We don't make plans over the path
we consider right, Emir Mansur. We just don't want Ascalon to fall
into the hands of the Crusaders. You can obey anybody you like
after that. We either save Ascalon
all together... ...our your obstinacy costs you
both your life and your homeland. Let us see how they'll help, father. Let's see if our enemy helps us
or harms us. My sister's right, father. We need even one person right now. Plus, they're the Border Veterans. Each of them is worth three knights. Our men around Ascalon said
they didn't see Saladin. Maybe he changed his mind
after he saw the siege. Recall those idiots immediately. Saladin must've already
entered Ascalon. Nureddin Zengi didn't send
his armies. Saladin came here with his men. What can he do to us with a few men? It doesn't matter how many there are. Saladin must've come with a plan. But he will fail. We're moving on to the big attack. All knights should be ready. Saladin checked our king. We'll checkmate him. Apart from the soldiers
guarding the entrance gate... ...with our defensive forces,
led by Halil... ...we'll defend
the eastern city wall... ...because the eastern city wall
is the heart of this city. It'll be a mistake to set the whole
defense at the eastern city wall. The Templar's will land
on the southern coast. We have to take position there. It'll be hard for them to make
a landing on the southern coast. They can't risk that. They know exactly you think that,
so they'll risk it. It's dangerous to weaken the defence
on the southern city wall. How will we risk that? If they can advance outside the
city wall, it'll be hard to resist. We'll destroy them with a sudden raid
before they can approach the wall The ships will try to enter the city
through the port. There's no need to attack them. It'll just be enough to put up a
line of defence and keep them off. We have a few ships. Our men
will put up a line of defence. We'll need your men at the coast. Our men will do what's necessary
at the sea. Listen, we complied with your plan
to take position at the coast. But stay off the ship plan. We live in the heart of the sea. You can't know naval battle
better than us, can you? We fought the Crusaders everywhere. You can't know how to fight them
better than us. Do you want to play the hero... ...or to save Ascalon? I want--
-Okay. Let's do as he says. Prepare the ships. Since the coastal raid
is important... ...go there with your comrades,
Sir Melik. I'll command the line of defence
at the sea. You can't be alone.
Şehinşah will come with you. Don't you break
the line of defence, Turanşah. No matter what happens,
do not leave your position. -Yes, Sir Melik.
-Yes, Sir Melik. Look how miserable we are, brother. What do you mean miserable, Esma? We've been trying to protect Ascalon
from one enemy... ...but you've let another enemy
be among us. And you're doing
anything Zengi's son tells you to do. If something happens to us
because of them... ...I'll never forgive that. Hold on. Do you think this is the Zengid land
that you try to rule the roost? We won't have that
spoilt Melik attitude here. Think about Ascalon
as much as you think about yourself. At least as much as that spoilt Melik
that you believe is the enemy. Apparently,
your father doesn't care about it. He just made a grandstand play
by sending Border Veterans here. If he was genuine,
he'd have sent the Zengid army. Our army would've been here... ...if it wasn't for your attempted
assassination in Jerusalem. You don't even know
what you did cost. But don't worry. When we crush the Templar's
and save Ascalon... ...we'll have cleaned your mess. Sir, they're moving the ships. So they're putting up
a line of defence at the sea. The knights on the should will
aim at the enemy ships at the bow... ...and wait for my sign.
-Yes, Sir. Wave the banner. Put up your shields! Come on! All ships! Light up the arrows! Shoot! I told you. Draw! Shoot! Shoot! Shoot! Come on! Attack! Shoot! Shoot! Attack! Attack! Attack! Attack! Attack! Come on! Ya Allah! The men at the coast
walked into an ambush! Saladin figured it out. You're good at chess, Saladin. But you haven't seen my move yet. Come here! Hurry. Sir, they galleys have started
to catch fire. We're retreating. Watch out! Watch out! Retreat! Retreat! Hurry, to the upper deck. Hurry! To the front! Come on! They're retreating. Now it's time to go at them. Lower the oars! -Come on!
-Come on! Come on, guys! Sir Melik told us not to leave our
positions, brother. Didn't you hear? Don't disobey the order! This isn't disobeying the order. I'm fulfilling my responsibilities. If we let them go, they'll regroup
and come at us. We'll attack and capsize them all
while they're weak. To the enemy ships! Let's go! Come on! Row! Pull! Pull! We can't leave him alone. Lower your oars! To the enemy ships! -Come on!
-Hurry! Hurry up! Stronger! Pull your oars! Come on! Sir Melik! What are your men doing? I told them not to move.
Why are they going towards them? Obviously,
the enemy galleys are broken down. They'll deliver a final blow.
-What blow? Gabriel's drawing them to himself
on purpose, don't you see? They'll fall into a trap. Come on. Stay. We'll weaken here. They'll die if I don't go. Stay here.
-As you order. Stay here. Come on, man. They're approaching, Sir. Yes. They're approaching their death. How could you move? How? Faster! Come on. Faster. Their fire couldn't burn us. Now it's time for our fire. Greek fire. Melik Saladin's here, Bey. -Sir Melik?
-How could you move, Şehinşah? They're drawing you into
a Greek fire, don't you see? I told that to my brother Turanşah... ...but I didn't want to leave him
alone when he went by himself. Turanşah. I'll call him to account
for disobeying my order. Signal the others! Everybody, to the oars! Pull! We're going back, come on! Come on! Come on! Faster! Come on! Pull! Pull! Come on! Pull! Faster! Saladin. Sir, Saladin made it in time. They're going back before
we could ignite the Greek fire. They've fallen into my clutches now. They can't get out of here. Signal. Have all the archers on the ships
be ready. Shoot! Put up your shields! Come on! Our traverse speed's been stalled.
We're trapped, Sir Melik. What do you think
you're doing, Ayyub? Huh? We kept this wet nurse thing hidden
so the secret wouldn't get out. Do you know what will happen
if this gets out? All hell will break loose
in the dynasty. It isn't time
for this kind of weakness... ...as we're taking
firm steps towards our goals. Is Saladin was so important... ...why did you throw him
into the fire then? Saladin disobeyed the order... ...and went to Ascalon. Şehinşah and Turanşah
are with him, too. How could you not stop him? What was I supposed to do?
Chain him to the palace? Don't you know that
he doesn't listen to anybody... ...when he sets his mind
on something? I gave my son away to you, so that
he'd be the foundation of the state. For greater causes. For Jerusalem. I'm keen on my goals. Then why aren't you with Saladin? Why isn't our army with him? You left them in the middle of hell
by themselves. And Saladin will be punished
for disobeying a state order... ...when he returns? He went there risking everything. If something... ...bad happens to Saladin
or his brothers in Ascalon... ...or if the state
punishes Saladin... ...I'll expose this secret. I'll tell that Saladin... ...is my son to everybody. We have to respond, Sir Melik. Or we won't be able to
get out of here. There flames raining on us,
not now, Şehinşah. If not, then never. Şehinşah, wait! Şehinşah! Şehinşah! Şehinşah! Hang in there, Şehinşah. Hang in there. We're going to
get out of here. Hang in there. They're really cornered, Sir. It's just the time to finish them. We burned them with our fire. Now we'll blow them up. They won't even find
your pieces, Saladin. -Protect Sir Melik!
-Put out the flames! Come on! I don't know when I'll come back
from the Border Veterans, mother. I entrust you with my son Ömer. I know what it feels like... ...to give away a child, whose scent
you haven't got enough of yet. I will... ...entrust you... ...with a son now. Yusuf. He didn't die, Şehinşah. Melik Saladin,
whom you're going to be with... ...is your own brother. -Mother.
-You'll understand ...when the time comes I trust your discreetness the most
among my children. Unite your soul with your brother's. And your blood with his. Keep the seal
on your mouth and heart. Take this secret to your grave. I'll save you from this
circle of fire. Don't worry, brother. We won't be able to get out
from this circle of fire. But I'll put out the fire that's been
burning inside me for years.

title: Barbaros: Sword of the Mediterranean
transcript_text:
INSPIRED BY REAL PEOPLE
AND EVENTS IN HISTORY. BARBAROSSA:
SWORD OF THE MEDITERRANEAN According to the legends... ...this realm called Mediterranean
by Turks... ...and Bahri Sefid by Arabians... ...is a monster that devoured
the crowns of such great kings... ...and so many
thrones of mighty sultans. Even Rome's Great Kaiser Cesar
had fallen into pirate hands... ...and was imprisoned
as a galley slave. Even the cavalrymen who came from
the far end of the Ancient World... ...on horseback riding high
in the saddle had to face... ...with the relentless surges. However, they never accepted
this place as the root of evil... ...but a bosom of a mother. One by one, they conquered
the fortresses in this place... ...which they would call
"The Aegean Sea" later on. Lesvos was one of those
conquered fortresses. One of the soldiers who fought
for the cause, settled there. He had four sons. The ones who couldn't see
the truth said that... ..."How can four sons
deal with this sea... ...which devoured
thousand kingdoms?" They shouldn’t have. Four brothers who sailed
through the storm of evil... ...calmed the cataclysms
chained the thunderstorms... ...and left their marks
to the seas. The sea which defeated many,
kneeled before them. They shed their blood to the sea. They became blood-brothers
with high tides. The one who sailed first into the
Mediterranean was called Baba Oruç. Years later, two brothers
were known by the name... ...Barbarossas. Yet, there was still time for them
to become pirates... ...and sultans in this sea. The uncharted story of
the famous legend had just begun. Reef the sails in! Captain has been suffering in pain
since we set sail. I told him thousand times.
Oleaster is the remedy. He never listens. He wanted me, I said okay. Now, it has become my duty
to be the captain... ...on top of guarding the cargo. Valiant levents. I have never lied to you. Never sent you to any trouble
that I didn't lead. I will be honest today as well. The waters are calm but shallow. Our duty is heavy, so is our cargo. Our goal is to survive the pass. But there may be a battle
ahead of us in our fate, too. Mankind is of three types.
First, are the kings. Second, the ones who
tremble before the kings. And those whom the kings fear. Thank Allah
that we've never been afraid. My hearties... ...those who'll stand in our way may
they be thugs or may they be kings... ...I swore I'll make them fear. May our oath be our fate? May our oath be our fate!
May my life end serving you! May our oath be our fate!
May my life end serving you! May our oath be our fate!
May my life end serving you! May our oath be our fate!
May my life end serving you! May our oath be our fate!
May my life end serving you! Hey! Heyamol! Ya mol, heyamol, mola heyamol! Ya mol, heyamol, heya heyamol! Let's start with Allah's name! Hele sayalesa, hele sayalesa Let's get down to work
and get this done! Hele sayalesa, hele sayalesa Hard to starboard! Hard to starboard! The gifts of the Ottoman Sultan
to the Mamluks will cross here. That ship has such a crew
that we don't want them... ...to be ready after seeing us. He said Sultan of Ottomans. It seems like these villains
will sink a Muslim ship. Let's be on our guards. Skipper, if they smell a rat,
blood will be shed. So be it.
We have lost our ship. Moreover, our freedom is gone. Does it matter
if we lose our lives, too? You talk too much Captain Vefa.
Too much. It is time for you to rest. See what happens to anyone
who tries to be a hero. Muffle the lot of them. Considering the echoes
coming from the rocks... ...the pass is too narrow. I'm not worried about
the pass anymore. But we are yet to see the end of it. Heya, heyamol! Hey! Bismillah. You know much about the birds,
Birdman. Isn't that a seagull?
-It is, Baba Oruç. As far as I know,
seagulls flock over the ships... ...to find some food
once they see it. Isn't that so?
-It is. It is, Baba Oruç. The sea is full of fish,
yet, they come for your food. 'Cuz it's easier. Since when seagulls have
stopped being greedy? We got the signal.
They entered the narrow pass. They must be seen
around the rocks soon. -İlyas.
-I'm here. Your orders? The deck should be dry.
We don't want to slip. Looks like the swords
will be unsheathed. Ambassador Hayrabay. Go back to your cabin.
Keep your guards close to you. The cannons will fire
as soon as we see their ship. It's best if they stop.
If they don't... Everyone, take cover. Silence. Shush! Ship ahoy! The ship is on the bow!
The ship is on the bow! Ağa Captain!
They also carry a cargo. Looks like a cargo ship but
Allah knows what villains it carries. They made the seagull sounds. They have sent the word
for others to be ready. Let's be on guard. Baba Oruç, it looks like
we will get abreast. They'll corner us in the pass
if we turn back. There is no place for us to slip out. The battle is inevitable now. Ya Allah, ya bismillah! Alesta! Alesta! Cannoneer, to the cannons. Hard to starboard! Hard to starboard! Reef the sails in! Get ready the brails! Fire! Hold on! Hold on! Helmsman, center! Hold on! We are centered! Bismillah, fire! Bismillah, fire! Bring the bows, it's time to clash! They're attacking back! Shoot your arrows! Now! Get ready to collide! Fire! Throw the hooks!
Throw 'em! Pull the ropes! Pull 'em! Cut the ropes! Attack! Give us the Ambassador
and the gifts of the Ottomans... ...and save your lives! They're gifts of
the Sultan of the Land. Not suitable for a
cutthroat pirate like you! I'm the Sultan of the Seas!
Come and get your gift from me! Sultan of the Seas, you say. I can be the Sultan a thousand times
in these waters that you pollute! Die then! Attack! Run, come on! Run! Run, run! Getting ambushed in such a place... ...must be a great treachery. It's not treachery,
great Ambassador. It's trade. Go check on Hayrabay. This is treason!
The worst kind of treason! Curse your fate, Master Hayrabay. You rats! Cold steel is the price to pay
for treason in our ship. Go on! Go! Come here, you infidel! I will avenge your past and
future victims with one blow. You'll pay for shedding
innocent blood. I'm gonna hang you
like a flag on this ship... ...so that you and your kind
won't shed innocent blood again. -Get up, get up!
-All right, okay! Why do you lay among the corpses? Spare my life. I never harmed anyone. It doesn't suit a man to cry off
in hardships. Forgive me.
Besides, I didn't kill anyone! It takes ages for you to put in
gunpowder and kill him, Cannoneer. Leave him, Birdman. Never mind. He is injured.
He can't survive the sea. He should thank İlyas' mercy. It's up to the mercy of the sea
from now on. Brother İlyas,
your kind heart is going to... ...bring us trouble one day.
We'll see... We fooled around a lot.
Come on, let's go! LESVOS Kalimera, İshak Bey.
Who are you waiting for? My brother will be back today. Good for you, sir.
Which one will be back? Who do you think, man?
Hızır will be back. Hızır. Are you treading on his toes
on purpose by asking that? What did I say? Baba Oruç and İlyas obviously
won't return to Lesvos! -Are you here too, Niko?
-Take this seat. I'm here too, İshak Ağa.
I'm here too. Well, it is your brother
but also my brother at heart. Why not! Bring in all Lesvos. We should make a welcome
ceremony with cannon fires, too! Keep off the edge, come here! Come. You jaw at us when you can't
find anyone to do so. So what?
We have been waiting for so long. Yes, dad. I was a baby when
my uncle went to Thessaloniki. And you think you're a grown up now?
You little rascal! Well, he will come home anyway. You don't have to be here.
-Then you should have waited at home. You were frantic,
saying that Hızır is coming. But you're very happy
that your brother is back though. You don't say it
but your face gives you away. Come on! You're ear bashing me.
You wore my joy thin. Just remain silent
if you want to wait. Okay. I’m fine.
Get this off here. Come on man. It was just a joke. I was about to be crushed
under my own stuff. I'm sorry, Captain Kostas. They say the sea is infested... ...with pickpocketing rats
these days. Thought you were one of them. Hızır, you have become
a good tradesman... ...but your strength and
your intelligence shows that... ...you're the descendant
of Yakup Ağa. Tradesmen don't have
the agility you have. What's up with it? Tell me, how am I like? Just like Baba Oruç,
the mighty lions of the seas. You said it yourself. My name is Hızır.
It literally means "Godsend". I have no business with sword.
I mean I'm not like Baba Oruç. If you say so... What is it? It looks like you got
valuable things in your pouch. You guard them for your life.
And you don't let the crew carry it. It bears the weight of science. I'm the only one who can carry it.
-Bless you, Hızır. Your tongue is sharper
than any pirate's sword. You don't have to
reach for your sword. Ship ahead!
İshak Ağa, Hızır is coming. -Hızır.
-İshak Ağa, Hızır is coming! The ship is ahead!
-My uncle is back! -Hızır is coming!
-Okay, okay, I'm not blind. Stop wriggling like fish
fresh out of the water. My uncle is back! Did you miss here? Of course, I did. It's homesickness. It breaks the heart
when you’re away... ...but it warms the heart
when you’re close by. Hard aport! -Selamünaleyküm, Captain Oruç.
-Aleykümselam. But no need to call me captain.
I don't even have a small galley. I wanted to call you so when
I heard your brother İlyas said it. He says so because
a three masted galleon... ...is our childhood dream. He keeps saying
Ağa Captain since then. Everyone else calls me Baba Oruç.
-All right, Baba Oruç. I came here to thank you. -Why?
-I didn't know... ...I nursed vipers to be
ambassadors one day. You saved me from their treason... ...and Mamluk State's pride to be
trampled on by the Mediterranean. You drew sword for us. I got my money
from Unita to guard you. I'll gladly face death for that cause
if necessary. Do you thank the baker after
buying a bread from him? Actually, I do. I like you Hayrabay. Thank you. Let me check around. What's up, Birdman? Did you reserve the cut of our
martyred brothers for their families? That was the first thing I did,
but we don't have much left. Three gold coins and
this copper wrist guard. It is okay.
That's meant for them. Everyone got their share, Baba Oruç.
Only you left. How about him? Yeah. Him and you. Here. Thank you. This is meant for me.
Thank you. LESVOS Uncle! You grew up so fast! Uncle, we missed you!
Glad you're here! I missed you too, lad.
I missed you too. Come on, rascals!
Let's see what I got for you. Here are your candies. Thank you, uncle. Don't eat all of it.
Give your friends some. Brother. Welcome, Hızır. Welcome. Thank you, brother. -Welcome, Hızır.
-Sister-in-law. Well-received. My brother!
-Welcome. Well-received. What is it? You carried books
again the whole way? You can't get enough of Master
Süleyman's complex books... ...ever since you were a boy. A tradesman's business
should always be... ...with the accounting book only. I really can't understand
how come you love reading... ...all of them like philosophers!
That's beyond me. They are entrusted to me.
I safeguard them. You will be undone
if you can't delve into... ...the mysteries of the world
with Master Süleyman! If you'd excuse me... Go, Hızır. I welcome the man
who knows where his home is. Go. Master Süleyman is our dad's friend.
I respect him but... ...you're my real master. My place is by your side.
Our father's home. I knew it. I always knew that you wouldn't
turn out like Oruç or İlyas. Go.
-All right. ALEXANDRIA Get lost! Go bark in your own yard! Who do you think you are
to kick us out? -Hey!
-Golem! I don't want to see them anymore.
-Leave! Where were we? Yes, listen. A ship will depart soon.
It will go near Sicilly. Olive harvest was good
this year there. You have money, but if you want more
I say invest in this ship. I'm up for
100 Venetian ducas, Isabel. And 50 Spanish reales from me. One of the 100 shares
of the Basca for you. And half a share for you. You can claim your share
once the goods arrive and are sold. Don't you know how much
a real is worth, Isabel? What's half a share? Its worth varies.
Different here as well. We get to decide what money
is worth what around here. Wheat from the villages
near the shore. Got 'em on the harbour. -How many sacks?
-So many. I'm not smart enough to count all. Good. It'll be worth
much during famine. I'll make the calculations
and strike a bargain. You just bring the men here. We got valuable goods, Isabel. We want the guard duties
from men of your choosing... ...before we depart from Alexandria. I will assign
my most trusted guards to you. They should be back in a day or two.
You can leave at once then. You got a deal, Isabel. Your most trusted guards you say? I was your most trusted once. Everyone makes mistakes. Yes. It was a mistake too.
Don't I deserve forgiveness? I don't mean you, Cafer. It was my mistake to trust you. I still can't forgive
myself at times. Direna! Tell the cook
that I want a good tajin of lamb... ...with plums and almonds.
Oruç will be back soon. They must have missed a good feast. I'm pregnant, right? Come on, tell me, Asiye Teyze. I'm pregnant,
I'm not making it up, right? I'm Asiye of many years
and you're pregnant, Despina. -Thank God!
-With a son, in fact. May God bless you, Asiye Teyze! You gave the world to me! You'll give Baba Oruç good news
when he is back. I better tidy up. What is it? Won't you tell him? Let's wait a few more weeks.
Just to be sure. Allah Allah. Well, we tried before but
God tested us every time... ...and didn't grant us one. I wouldn't want to get
Oruç's hopes up for nothing. I think you should tell him. Seamen are hard to domesticate.
They may wander about. Even if this is wrong news
it will keep his mind on you. Don't you worry. Don't tarnish my house with other
people's lies. One word of Oruç was enough for me
to leave behind my father's home. If he up and leaves me,
I'll say that he wasn't mine. Say that it was a dream
and then leave.

title: Journey to Mecca
transcript_text: mekka sits in the western mountains of Saudi Arabia in a sanctuary roughly 100 miles square since long before Islam it has been considered sacred territory where no one could hunt cut trees or fight at the heart of the city is a great mosque called al-masjid al-haram and at its center is the Kaaba Muslims don't worship the Kaaba they worship what it represents the One God Hajj as duplicitous Jews as connivers Jews as uh pulling the strings uh and pitting people against each other Jews as Eternal enemies who can never be trusted uh this hits basically uh every single one
